name: Cisco IOS XR Upgrade
description: Upgrade a Cisco IOS XR device using the install add/activate workflow. Optional SMU batches may be applied before and/or after the version upgrade. Restart type (reload vs process-restart) is auto-detected per SMU. Select target devices in the Job Template's Inventory or Limit field.

spec.question_description: Version string that must match EXACTLY what `show install active summary` prints on the Label: line after upgrade (e.g. 24.4.2 for a 3-octet release, 24.4.2.1 for a respin). Used in the post-check assertion.
Filesystem where the image and SMUs are staged.
TAR bundle or ISO filename already staged on the device (e.g. asr9k-x64-24.4.2.1.tar). Must exist at the chosen image path above.
32-char hex hash of the image file. Verified on-device before install. Generate with `md5sum <filename>` on the file server.
Patches the CURRENT image before the version jump. Committed immediately so they survive a rollback. Leave empty to skip.

One line per SMU, format: filename: md5hash. REQUIRED when SMUs BEFORE is non-empty. Example:
  asr9k-sysadmin-install-7.8.2.2-r782.CSCwi95358.x86_64.rpm: 6cb3873222ec7803ab7e2107551f3f5c
Applied on top of the NEW image. Committed atomically with the version upgrade at the end. Leave empty to skip.
One line per SMU, format: filename: md5hash. REQUIRED when SMUs AFTER is non-empty.
Remove core files and inactive packages from XR + admin planes before upgrade. Recommended.
Disable FPD auto-upgrade/auto-reload before the image install so FPD changes are controlled in the FPD phase.
After image install, upgrade all FPDs (sequenced first if listed, then bulk).
Run `admin hw-module location all reload` after FPD upgrades. Required for BIOS-class FPD changes.
Max wait for SSH to return after a reload. Increase for large chassis.
Pause between POST checks passing and `install commit`. Window to observe and abort.
Where pre/post state snapshots are saved. Use a persistent mount to keep across job runs.
